title: Decoding the phenomenology of spontaneous thought using large language-model ratings on verbal retrospective free reports
abstract:
A bstract

Spontaneous thoughts constitute most of everyday inner experience, yet long-standing methodological challenges obscure a thorough exploration of their content and neurophysiological underpinnings. Traditional approaches relying on thought probes impose strict constraints on phenomenological reports, whereas online verbal reports disrupt the natural flow of experience while interfering neural signals with motor artifacts. Here, we designed and tested an alternative approach to assess the neural basis of spontaneous thoughts combining delayed verbal retrospective free reports (RFR) with automated phenomenological ratings generated by large language models (LLMs). Twenty-two participants performed an eyes-closed free-thinking task, providing reports that were evaluated along ten phenomenological dimensions by four state-of-the-art LLMs and a panel of human raters. Machine-learning models (ML) were then trained to decode LLM-derived ratings from EEG spectral, complexity, and connectivity features. Our analyses showed that inter-rater agreement among LLMs exceeded that of human raters whereas ML models achieved above-chance accuracy for the prediction of emotional valence. These findings provide support for the use of LLMs for a scalable phenomenological annotation of spontaneous thoughts and suggest that their affective dimensions can be decoded from concurrent EEG activity.
ai_for_science_reason: The paper uses LLMs and machine learning to decode and analyze spontaneous thoughts, contributing to neuroscience by exploring the neural basis of these thoughts.
implementation_justification: The study implements LLMs and ML models effectively to analyze EEG data.
review_justification: The paper provides a novel approach but lacks extensive comparison with existing methods.
benchmark_justification: There is limited benchmarking against other phenomenological annotation methods.
hypothesis_generation_justification: The study suggests new hypotheses about the neural correlates of spontaneous thoughts.
scientific_discovery_justification: The research advances understanding of the neural basis of spontaneous thoughts.
experiment_design_justification: The experiment is well-designed to test the neural correlates of spontaneous thoughts using LLMs.
material_discovery_justification: The study does not involve material discovery.
molecular_design_justification: The study does not involve molecular design.
drug_discovery_justification: The study does not involve drug discovery.
causal_discovery_justification: The study explores potential causal relationships between EEG features and thought phenomenology.
biomedical_discovery_justification: The research contributes to understanding brain function related to spontaneous thoughts.
idea_development_justification: The paper develops the idea of using LLMs for phenomenological annotation in neuroscience.
paper_title: Decoding the phenomenology of spontaneous thought using large language-model ratings on verbal retrospective free reports

objective.answer: The primary objective of the paper is to develop and evaluate a combined approach that integrates retrospective free reports (RFR), automated large language model (LLM)-based phenomenological rating, and machine-learning EEG decoding to probe the neural correlates of spontaneous thought content.
objective.evidence: The current study aims to develop and evaluate a combined approach that integrates RFR paradigm, automated LLM-based phenomenological rating, and machine-learning EEG decoding as an ecologically feasible means of probing the neural correlates of spontaneous thought content.

knowledge_gap.answer: The paper addresses the gap in understanding the full phenomenological content of spontaneous thought and its mapping onto neural activity, which is constrained by traditional thought-probe paradigms.
knowledge_gap.evidence: However, most of these studies relied on thought-probe paradigms that constrain reports to predefined categories, leaving open the question of how the full phenomenological content of spontaneous thought maps onto neural activity.

answer: The use of large language models (LLMs) for automated phenomenological ratings of spontaneous thoughts.
Combining retrospective free reports (RFR) with LLM-derived ratings and EEG decoding to study spontaneous thought.
The integration of machine-learning models to decode LLM-derived ratings from EEG features.
Achieving higher inter-rater agreement among LLMs compared to human raters.
evidence: Our strategy combined the RFR paradigm with automated ratings of verbal reports of spontaneous thoughts generated by four commercially available large language models (LLMs).
The current study aims to develop and evaluate a combined approach that integrates RFR paradigm, automated LLM-based phenomenological rating, and machine-learning EEG decoding.
Machine-learning models (ML) were then trained to decode LLM-derived ratings from EEG spectral, complexity, and connectivity features.
Our analyses showed that inter-rater agreement among LLMs exceeded that of human raters.

inspirational_papers.answer:
- Andrews-Hanna et al. (2013) Dimensions of self-generated thought content and relationships with individual differences in emotional wellbeing. (Methodological precursors)
- Smallwood et al. (2016) Representing representation: Integration between the temporal lobe and the posterior cingulate influences the content and form of spontaneous thought. (Experimental baselines)
inspirational_papers.evidence: The contents of spontaneous thought vary along several dimensions, such as temporal orientation (past, present, or future), emotional valence, self-relevance, sensory modality (verbal versus imagery), deliberateness, and vividness (Andrews-Hanna et al., 2013, 2022; Kirberg et al., 2025; Stawarczyk et al., 2011, 2013). Experience sampling studies have further shown that distinct patterns of phenomenological features cluster into separable cognitive states, such as goal-directed planning, ruminative self-reflection, and perceptual engagement (Coppola et al., 2024; Karapanagiotidis et al., 2017; Konu et al., 2021).

steps.step: Participants engage in a free-thinking task followed by retrospective verbal reports.
steps.evidence: Participants performed a free-thinking task eyes closed (30-second trials), followed by immediately providing RFRs describing the content of their thoughts.

steps.step: Transcription and phenomenological rating of verbal reports.
steps.evidence: Audio recordings were transcribed verbatim, while EEG data underwent standard preprocessing including filtering (1-90 Hz), artifact removal, and feature extraction.
Transcribed reports were evaluated along 10 phenomenological dimensions using both human raters and Large Language Models (LLMs).

steps.step: EEG data preprocessing and feature extraction.
steps.evidence: EEG data preprocessing was performed using the MNE-Python toolbox.

steps.step: Machine learning model training to decode EEG features.
steps.output: Decoding of phenomenological dimensions from EEG.
steps.evidence: A machine-learning pipeline was implemented to decode each phenomenological dimension from resting-state EEG features.

tools.name: ChatGPT-5.2
tools.evidence: Multiple state-of-the-art LLMs (ChatGPT-5.2, DeepSeek-V3, MiMo, Gemini-3-Flash) performed automated ratings using identical phenomenological definitions and rating scales.

tools.name: MNE-Python toolbox
tools.evidence: EEG data preprocessing was performed using the MNE-Python toolbox.

evaluation_metrics.name: Spearman’s rank correlation coefficient
evaluation_metrics.purpose: Measures agreement between LLM ratings and human consensus.
evaluation_metrics.application: Used to assess the concordance of LLM ratings with human raters.
evaluation_metrics.evidence: Agreement between each LLM and the human consensus was then assessed by computing Spearman’s rank correlation coefficient (ρ).

evaluation_metrics.name: Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC)
evaluation_metrics.purpose: Measures the performance of the machine learning model in decoding EEG features.
evaluation_metrics.application: Used to evaluate the classification performance of the machine learning model.
evaluation_metrics.evidence: Model performance was assessed using ROC-AUC with permutation testing for statistical validation.

methods.name: Experimental design generation
methods.description: The approach includes producing experimental protocols, configurations, or evaluation strategies.
methods.evidence: Participants performed a free-thinking task eyes closed (30-second trials), followed by immediately providing RFRs describing the content of their thoughts.

methods.name: Knowledge Extraction and Structurization
methods.description: Extracting structured knowledge from unstructured data.
methods.evidence: Audio recordings were transcribed verbatim, while EEG data underwent standard preprocessing including filtering (1-90 Hz), artifact removal, and feature extraction.

areas.name: Health Sciences
areas.description: The study explores the neural correlates of spontaneous thought, which is relevant for understanding psychiatric conditions.
areas.evidence: Understanding the neural basis of spontaneous thought is relevant for both fundamental and clinically applied neuroscience, as alterations in this process have been associated with psychiatric conditions such as depression, anxiety, and attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD).

areas.name: Applied Sciences & Engineering
areas.description: The study involves the application of machine learning and EEG signal processing.
areas.evidence: Machine-learning models (ML) were then trained to decode LLM-derived ratings from EEG spectral, complexity, and connectivity features.

performance_summary.summary: LLMs achieved higher inter-rater agreement than human raters, and the machine learning model decoded emotional valence from EEG with above-chance accuracy.
performance_summary.evidence: Our analyses showed that inter-rater agreement among LLMs exceeded that of human raters whereas ML models achieved above-chance accuracy for the prediction of emotional valence.

baselines.name: Human Rater Consistency
baselines.description: Used as a baseline for comparing LLM performance.
baselines.evidence: All four LLMs surpassed the Human Rater Consistency baseline.

benchmark_datasets.name: Multidimensional Experience Sampling (MDES)
benchmark_datasets.data_description: Phenomenological dimensions for evaluating spontaneous thought.
benchmark_datasets.usage: Used as a framework for evaluating phenomenological dimensions.
benchmark_datasets.evidence: The evaluated dimensions included: Valence, Arousal, Past/Future, Present/NotPresent, Self-centeredness, Imaginary/Real, Verbal/Imagery Modality, Internally/Externally Triggered, Vague/Detailed, and Functionality/Goal Oriented.

evaluation_metrics.name: Spearman’s rank correlation coefficient
evaluation_metrics.purpose: Measures agreement between LLM ratings and human consensus.
evaluation_metrics.application: Used to assess the concordance of LLM ratings with human raters.
evaluation_metrics.evidence: Agreement between each LLM and the human consensus was then assessed by computing Spearman’s rank correlation coefficient (ρ).

evaluation_metrics.name: Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC)
evaluation_metrics.purpose: Measures the performance of the machine learning model in decoding EEG features.
evaluation_metrics.application: Used to evaluate the classification performance of the machine learning model.
evaluation_metrics.evidence: Model performance was assessed using ROC-AUC with permutation testing for statistical validation.

benchmark_dataset.name: Multidimensional Experience Sampling (MDES)
benchmark_dataset.data_description: Phenomenological dimensions for evaluating spontaneous thought.
benchmark_dataset.usage: Used as a framework for evaluating phenomenological dimensions.
benchmark_dataset.evidence: The evaluated dimensions included: Valence, Arousal, Past/Future, Present/NotPresent, Self-centeredness, Imaginary/Real, Verbal/Imagery Modality, Internally/Externally Triggered, Vague/Detailed, and Functionality/Goal Oriented.

limitations.name: Temporal Resolution
limitations.description: The RFR paradigm cannot resolve rapid fluctuations within a trial, potentially missing brief mental episodes.
limitations.evidence: Its most fundamental constraint is temporal: by collecting a single retrospective report for each 30-second interval, the approach cannot resolve rapid fluctuations within a trial.

limitations.name: Memory Bias
limitations.description: Retrospective reports depend on memory, which may introduce a sampling bias.
limitations.evidence: Retrospective reports depend on memory. Thoughts that are more important, deliberate, or future-oriented are recalled preferentially.

future_directions.name: Individualized Modeling
future_directions.description: Explore individualized modeling approaches to account for inter-individual variability.
future_directions.evidence: The variability across participants points to a need for individualized modeling approaches in future work.

future_directions.name: Higher-Density Recordings
future_directions.description: Use high-density EEG or concurrent fMRI recordings to improve spatial resolution.
future_directions.evidence: High-density EEG or concurrent fMRI recordings could also improve spatial resolution and access to medial structures implicated in self-referential and temporal processing.

resource_link.evidence: Code and processed data are available at https://github.com/Nicobruno92/mw_free_speech.
